raw:
{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 0.7,
    "R1.3": 0.7,
    "R1.4": 0.3,
    "R1.5": 0.7,
    "R1.6": 1.0,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 1.0,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 1.0,
    "RX.4": 1.0,
    "RX.5": 1.0
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Do you want A) a light edit focused on sentence-level clarity and minor consistency (mainly R1.6 with limited R1.2/R1.3), or B) a substantive revision that can reframe the contribution, reorganize sections, and calibrate claims versus evidence (R1.1, R1.5, with deeper R1.2/R1.3 and limited R1.4/R1.7)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Reframing the thesis and contribution may be needed to clearly position the mechanism and its core insight.",
    "R1.2": "Ensuring the argument from motivation to method to conclusions is logically coherent is central to improving the draft.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about performance and ablations should be checked for alignment with the described evidence.",
    "R1.5": "Section-level ordering and narrative arc may require reorganization for a clearer flow from problem to results.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision are essential to elevate readability and precision.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should transparently indicate uncertainty and limits of the claims where appropriate.",
    "RX.2": "The revision must avoid overstating novelty or generalization beyond what is supported.",
    "RX.3": "All work should stay within the scope of improving this draft without expanding objectives.",
    "RX.4": "Language and positioning should avoid misleading readers or encouraging misuse of results.",
    "RX.5": "Changes should be traceable to the original text so the provenance of edits is clear."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Do you want A) a light edit focused on sentence-level clarity and minor consistency (mainly R1.6 with limited R1.2/R1.3), or B) a substantive revision that can reframe the contribution, reorganize sections, and calibrate claims versus evidence (R1.1, R1.5, with deeper R1.2/R1.3 and limited R1.4/R1.7)?

rationale.R1.1: Reframing the thesis and contribution may be needed to clearly position the mechanism and its core insight.
rationale.R1.2: Ensuring the argument from motivation to method to conclusions is logically coherent is central to improving the draft.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about performance and ablations should be checked for alignment with the described evidence.
rationale.R1.5: Section-level ordering and narrative arc may require reorganization for a clearer flow from problem to results.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence-level clarity, grammar, and concision are essential to elevate readability and precision.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should transparently indicate uncertainty and limits of the claims where appropriate.
rationale.RX.2: The revision must avoid overstating novelty or generalization beyond what is supported.
rationale.RX.3: All work should stay within the scope of improving this draft without expanding objectives.
rationale.RX.4: Language and positioning should avoid misleading readers or encouraging misuse of results.
rationale.RX.5: Changes should be traceable to the original text so the provenance of edits is clear.
